findings.description: The right-aligned header block containing official names and titles is duplicated.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the header block ('PAT McCRORY...', etc.) appears once at the top right. In the HTML, it appears at the top right, and is then duplicated below the injected footer elements, preceding the 'MEMORANDUM' title.

findings.description: The outline list at the bottom of the page is split, with parts appearing before and after an injected footer block.
findings.evidence: The outline list at the bottom of PDF page 1 contains four items. In the HTML, items I and II appear on page 1, while items III and IV appear at the top of page 2, separated from the rest of the list and immediately followed by injected footer elements.

findings.description: Document footer elements are injected into the middle of continuous text blocks, disrupting the reading order.
findings.evidence: At the transition between pages, the footer text and gradient bar are rendered inline, breaking sentences in half. For example, between PDF pages 2 and 3, the sentence 'Comments were made alleging that wildlife...' is split, with the footer elements appearing directly between 'made' and 'alleging' in the HTML.

findings.description: Page footer text and decorative footer images are inserted directly into the middle of sentences where page breaks occurred in the original PDF.
findings.evidence: In Section II, the 'Environmental' bullet is interrupted: 'Comments were made [FOOTER HTML] alleging that wildlife...'. A similar interruption occurs in Section III's 'Environmental' bullet: '...relying on water intakes [FOOTER HTML] downstream of the Belews Creek...'
